title: Bohemian Rhapsody I Exit Survey (Ep. 94)
format: Sean Fennessey and Rob Harvilla run an Exit Survey on Bohemian Rhapsody: a bad movie that gets by on the real Queen songs and a near shot-for-shot Live Aid finale, with a lip-synced Rami Malek turning in the best performance in it.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: Number one movie in the country, $140M worldwide over the weekend, and both hosts agree it's a bad one: drab, predictable biopic beats and a band-mandated insistence that all four members matter. Bryan Singer directed until he left mid-shoot; Dexter Fletcher finished it. The hosts note the abandoned Sacha Baron Cohen / Stephen Frears version would have been rawer but wouldn't have gotten the Queen catalog. Rami Malek is the best thing in it, lip-syncing rather than singing, and the roughly 20-minute Live Aid recreation is the reason to see it.

segments.heading: The rock biopic, before and after
segments.note: comparison points the hosts reach for

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Playing next door in theater 10; Harvilla wandered in mid-'Shallow' and cried before doubling back to Bohemian Rhapsody. The other rock movie of the fall.

films.title: Walk the Line
films.note: Fennessey rewatched it and thinks it has aged badly: Joaquin Phoenix sings Johnny Cash but 'sounds nauseous.' Their case against making the actor sing.

films.title: Get On Up
films.note: Chadwick Boseman doesn't sing the James Brown vocals and it doesn't matter, because the dancing carries it. Cited for why lip-sync can help.

films.title: This Is Spinal Tap
films.note: Harvilla's favorite of the form, if a mockumentary is allowed to count.

films.title: The Runaways
films.note: His favorite real-life rock biopic: trashy, lurid, unbothered by accuracy or award ambition. Floria Sigismondi directed; Michael Shannon plays Kim Fowley.

films.title: Last Days
films.note: Gus Van Sant's shapeless Kurt Cobain figure (Michael Pitt), made oblique because the estate wouldn't grant the real story.

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: Unnamed in the episode: the upcoming Taron Egerton Elton John film, directed by Dexter Fletcher, who finished this Queen movie. Egerton sings; Fennessey isn't sold on the footage.

films.title: Vox Lux
films.note: December's Natalie Portman-as-invented-rock-star film, one data point in the actors-playing-rock-stars trend the hosts flag.

films.title: Her Smell
films.note: Alex Ross Perry's Elisabeth Moss rock movie, the third in that same trend.
